HARDWARE.TITLE: Hardware Configuration
HARDWARE.I2C_BUS_NUMBER_DOC: I2C bus number — usually 1 on Raspberry Pi (default: 1)
HARDWARE.I2C_ADDRESS_DOC: I2C address of the OLED display (default: 0x3C)
HARDWARE.ROTATE_180_DOC: Rotate display 180 degrees for upside-down mounted displays (default: off)

DISPLAY.TITLE: Display Options
DISPLAY.SCROLL_SPEED_DOC: Text scroll speed in pixels per frame, 1 (slow) to 10 (fast) (default: 3)
DISPLAY.REFRESH_INTERVAL_DOC: Display refresh interval. Lower = smoother scrolling but higher CPU usage (default: 500ms)
DISPLAY.IDLE_DIM_SECONDS_DOC: Dim display after this many seconds of idle. Set to 0 to never dim (default: 120)
DISPLAY.CLOCK_24H_DOC: Use 24-hour clock format. When off, uses 12-hour with AM/PM (default: on)
DISPLAY.COLON_BLINK_DOC: Blink the colon on the idle screen clock, 1 second on / 1 second off (default: on)
DISPLAY.SCREENSAVER_MODE_DOC: Screensaver style activated after idle + dim timeout (default: Bouncing Clock)
DISPLAY.SCREENSAVER_SECONDS_DOC: Activate screensaver after this many seconds past the dim timeout. Set to 0 to never activate (default: 300)
DISPLAY.VOLUME_OVERLAY_SECONDS_DOC: How long the full-screen volume overlay displays when volume changes (default: 2)
DISPLAY.DATE_FORMAT_DOC: Date display format on the idle screen (default: Day + Month name)
